feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Does SAP DataSphere have a "Business Content" option like SAP BW for ETL processes?
entry.content:
Hi

In SAP BW, there is an option called "Install from Business Content," which provides a built-in module for the entire ETL (Extraction, Transformation, Loading) process for each InfoProviders . SD,MM,FI.... My question is, does SAP DataSphere have a similar feature where we can easily download and install readily available business content for ETL processes? If so, how can we leverage it for quick deployment?


Kumar

entry.title: Problem with Geo-coodinates colums
entry.content:
I request support, I managed to use the Geo-coodinates colums from the Datasphere and now when I try to view it again from the Datasphere the following message is displayed: Right now, there are no visible columns in the table. Please select the columns you need in the table settings.

The creation of the Location dimension is done as follows:
1. A graphical view called CRM_PropiedadeCroquiCliente_1 with the columns latitude and longitude is available.


2. Created a CRM_PropiedadeCroquiCliente_2 view with Semantic Usage "Dimension"


3. A Calculated Columns was created with Data Type hana. ST_Geometry from CRM_PropiedadeCroquiCliente_2

4. Created a CRM_PropiedadeCroquiCliente_3 view with Semantic Usage "Fact" and with Associations to CRM_PropiedadeCroquiCliente_2 by idparceiro


5. An Analytic Model was created CRM_PropiedadeCroquiClienteFinal_V1

entry.title: python script that uses oauth2.0 with 'api access' without manuel login to fetch data via odata?
entry.content:
Python script to get Datasphere data via odata api:

I have seen some guides to create oauth2.0 client and make rest calls to datasphere odata apis via Postman. However, that  oauth2.0 client uses the 'interactive' purpose option with a callback uri. That means i have to manually login on browser or open the link to get the first token and then continue querying via the odata api. This method works fine, however, it cannot be used for production script that pulls data from a Datasphere view every day.

For this reason, I tried with a oauthclient2.0 with 'api access' purpose and 'client_credentials'  as authorization, in order to avoid manual login. With the client_id and secret, i am able to call the token_url and receive a token. However, when i use this token to query any view or table in datasphere, it returns the login page html elements as text in the response.  
So my question is it possible to somehow avoid manual login and run a python script locally or in Azure cloud Function to get data from Datasphere via odata apis. 

@Asim_Munshi @gustavokath

entry.title: Issue where standard CDS View cannot be imported in Datasphere
entry.content:
Thank you for viewing.

As part of my investigation into Datasphere's hierarchy function, I am currently trying to reproduce the steps in the blog below on my actual device, but I am experiencing an issue where the CDS View related to G/L account information starting with "I_GLACCOUNT" in the blog is not displayed on Datasphere's remote table import screen. (Figure 1)

SAP Datasphere: Introducing the Directory Hierarchy - SAP Community 

 (図１)

 

By referring to the blog below, we have investigated the settings required for the target CDS View and confirmed that the following two conditions are met.
How to use CDS views that can extract data - SAP Community 
・The annotation "@Analytics.dataExtraction.enabled" is set to "true"
・"ISSAPRELEASEDVIEW" in I_DATAEXTRACTIONENABLEDVIEW is set to X (⇒It is a released CDS view that can extract data)

However, one thing that struck me while researching the above was that the CDS View starting with "I_GR" shown in the image in (Figure 1) was displayed on Datasphere even though "ISSAPRELEASEDVIEW" was not set to X. (The annotation "@Analytics.dataExtraction.enabled" was set to "true".)

 

I suspect that there is some setting that I've overlooked. if anyone knows how to solve this issue, I would appreciate your help.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: OData API request not working when Datasphere is set with SSO
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

I have an OData URL that I use to get data from my Analytical Model in Datasphere:

the URL is:  https://tcodataspherefree.eu10.hcs.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/TCO_DATA_MODEL/Landscape_daily_cost_capacity_metering/Landscape_daily_cost_capacity_metering?$top=10

It works just fine if I use it in my browser after I sign in with SSO.

But when I want to call it through a python code, after setting up OAuth on Datasphere, I am not getting any data back from the URL. I assume it is because the SSO is set.

Is there any way to get around that?

Many thanks

Sam

entry.title: Sharepoint CSV-File integration in Datasphere's Data Flow leads to an error
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

we are currently trying to establish a connection between SAP Datasphere and a customer's SharePoint.

To do this, we followed the instructions described in the following blog entry...

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/accessing-sharepoint-files-from-datasphere-using-btp-open-connectors/ba-p/13917779

...and successfully created the Integration Suite service, along with the SharePoint connector provided by the Open Connectors.

Here is the configuration of the Open Connectors pointing to Sharepoint:

 

Unfortunately, we have not yet been able to display a file in the Datasphere.

To do this, we created a "test.csv" with a handful of data, which is located in the "Documents/Datasphere_Test" directory.

We can already see this folder structure in the Dataflow of the Datasphere:

 

But as soon as we drag and drop the "test.csv" file in, we get the following error message:

 

The error message displayed, shows that the "test.csv" cannot be found.

The Open Connectors uses the following URL call, which leads to the error:

<company>.sharepoint.com/_api/web/GetFolderByServerRelativeUrl('Freigegebene%20Dokumente/Datasphere_Test/test.csv')

 

We have already experimented a bit here and tested via the browser that the file can be found via the following URL:

<company>.sharepoint.com/_api/Web/GetFileByServerRelativePath(decodedurl='/Freigegebene%20Dokumente/Datasphere_Test/test.csv')

We assume that we still need to define something in the Open Connectors configuration?

Is there perhaps a challenge with URL coding or something similar?

We would be grateful for any relevant information here.

Additional challenge:
The above named blog entry aims to ensure that we have access to the top level of the SharePoint. In the future, however, we also want to access sites, such as the site “Datalake” that we created here:

 

Here, too, the question arises  what we need to adjust the Open Connectors (or perhaps also in Sharepoint) so that we can also consume files that exist in the 'datalake' site.

 

Our attempt to adjust the Sharepoint site address in the form

<company>.sharepoint.com/sites/datalake

led to authentication errors.

 

If we leave the Sharepoint site address untouched (<company>.sharepoint.com), we can authenticate, but we cannot see the data structures of the site “datalake”.

 

Changing the “Document Library” property to

/sites/datalake/

was also unsuccessful.

 

Does anyone here have experience of how we need to adjust our configuration(s)?

 

Many thanks in advance,

M. Spyker

entry.title: Schedule on fix hour replication flow based on S/4HANA CDS View
entry.content:
Hi expert,

We are implemented a Datasphere project for our customer and facing an issue where we were not able to schedule the delta load for a replication flow.

We can launch init + Delta, select delta intervall (for expample every 24h), but not choose à fix hour during the night.

Do you know if this function will be available in datasphere ?

Thanks a lot,

Regards, Bruno

entry.title: Import of Package Sales Analysis failed - Status Objects Pending
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I want to import the Business Content Package "Sales Analysis for SAP S/4Hana and SAP S/4 Hana Cloud" into SAP Datasphere.

I already imported the space, connection, tables, and views successfully. Now that I created the replication flow and ran it successfully, I wanted to import the Analytic Models.

So I just selected the models in import options. But the import fails with the following messages (I also tried to select the space + the analytic models the second time):

 

Thanks in advance

Regards

Maike

entry.title: Tableau desktop user into allowlist IP's in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Datasphere community,

We have a use case where different tableau desktop users need to allowlist Ip's to connect to views in datasphere. 

Is there a better solution to allow list Ip's where users will be connecting from different locations and dont have to allowlist each time they change the location?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Can we add both Data structure and table to Sq01 Query?
entry.content:
I would like to add one structure and one table to the same query using Sq01.

These structure doesn`t have any table. So what should I do? Should I create the tables first and then add the queries or is there any other way?

entry.title: Error Loading 0FI_GL_4 Datasource to SAP Datasphere: "Disable Delta Capturing" Requirement
entry.content:
Dear experts 

I am trying to load the 0FI_GL_4 datasource from SAP S/4HANA into SAP Datasphere. However, I am encountering the following error:

 

Spoiler
To replicate an object that does not have a primary key using source connection type ABAP, you must first disable delta capturing for this table.

Is there a way to configure 0FI_GL_4 to work with delta capturing in Datasphere by adding a primary key or using a custom solution.

Priyanka

entry.title: Extend Datasphere Business content package.
entry.content:
Hello,
In BW you could make a copy of the Business content to extend it with more fields.
In the case of SAP Datasphere, what would be the recommendation? If we want to use the Sales package of the Business content, what would be the best practice to extend it?
I understand that if we extend the contents of the package directly, we would lose the changes in the case of a new import, right?
Thank you very much.

entry.title: Dynamically set default DATE value for input parameter in Datasphere or SAC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

  Is it possible to dynamically set default value of DATE variable for previous day(CURRENT_DATE - 1) using INPUT PARAMETER(Datasphere) or Variable(SAC) ?

Thanks a lot

entry.title: Getting Column name inconsistency error in Dataspehre
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I"m getting below error, when trying to deploy the subsequent flow:

Review the following errors:
"DF_Table1" - Table DF_Table1 inconsistent: Column "Month" in deployed model but not on DB,
Correlation ID: bd56da16-3216-4994-4fa0-73d14024a86

entry.title: How to Identify CDS Views as Alternatives to Classic Extractors for SAP Datasphere Integration
entry.content:
Dear Datasphere Experts

I’m currently working on integrating SAP S/4HANA with SAP Datasphere using ODP, as recommended by SAP. I understand that instead of the classic extractors (e.g., 2LIS_11_VAHDR, 0FI_GL_4), it’s now best practice to leverage CDS views.

Could someone guide me on how to:

Identify the relevant CDS views that correspond to these classic extractors?
Validate if these CDS views are ODP-enabled for extraction to SAP Datasphere?
Handle cases where no direct CDS view is available for a classic extractor?
Any tips, examples, or SAP resources (e.g., notes, documentation) would be greatly appreciated, especially for the specific examples like 2LIS_11_VAHDR and 0FI_GL_4.

Thank you for your insights

Priyanka

entry.title: Compatibility of SAP ECC version 6 EHP 5 with SAP Datasphere
entry.content: I would like to know if SAP Datasphere can be integrated with SAP ECC version 6.0 EHP 5

entry.title: Datasphere Replication flow - Delta load of LO or SAPI extractor from ECC
entry.content:
Dear Datasphere Experts,

This pertains to the extraction of LO extractors (2LIS*) from ECC to a third-party target, such as AWS, using a "replication flow" facilitated by premium outbound connectivity.

According to SAP documentation, only an initial load is supported when the source object lacks a primary key for ODP artifacts.

Reference: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/c8a54ee704e94e15926551293243fd1d/2267a9f6a5724ec0ae5632db4f9f70e5.html

Here are my questions:

  1. What are the possible workarounds for this limitation, considering LO extractors (2LIS*) are widely used across organizations, and delta functionality is essential?
  2. Is there a plan in SAP's roadmap (I couldn’t locate one) to provide delta functionality for all standard and custom extractions without primary key?

This is a common requirement as It work perfectly in SAP BW, and I believe it warrants attention. I would appreciate your guidance.

BR,

Yatendra

entry.title: SAP Datasphere replication flow projection filter for null/ Blank values.
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,
What is expression in Projection filter in SAP Datasphere replication flow for null/ Blank values. Any clue would be helpful.
Best Regards
Arvind

entry.title: Is there any possibility to convert a Hana Graphical Calculation view to SAP DataSphere view?
entry.content:
I have a new mission that is figure out a way to convert Hana Graphical view to SAP DataSphere view.

I did not find any doc or even comment about this procedure, so does any one had issue like this or have heard something that can help me. 

 

Thanks in Advance

entry.title: DAC not working in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi  All 

 

I been following this blog 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/introducing-new-type-of-data-access-control-quot-operator-and-value-quot/bc-p/13946785#M177241

I recreated the blog and my results are all good except for product_group = 2. 

my view

my DAC table 

 

my actual table 

what could be the issue , it only does not work for product_group = 2 ( it works for all other product groups)
